twitter_thread: The S&P 500's 1% dividend yield means $1000 invested earns just $10/yr.
Pipeline stocks like MPLX and EPD offer 5-7% yields, boosting returns.
VYM and SCHD ETFs hold 400+ dividend stocks, reducing risk.
Don't put more than 5% of your portfolio in a single high-yield stock.
Tax-efficient investing can add 1-2% to your annual returns.
REITs like Realty Income and National Retail offer 4-5% yields.
pack.linkedin: A $1000 investment in the S&P 500 earns just $10 per year with its 1% dividend yield. To boost returns, consider pipeline stocks like MPLX and EPD, which offer 5-7% yields, or ETFs like VYM and SCHD that hold 400+ dividend stocks. Tax-efficient investing can add 1-2% to your annual returns, and REITs like Realty Income and National Retail offer 4-5% yields. What's your go-to high-yield investment strategy?
tiktok_hooks: 1% yields are a joke. I'm earning 7% with these stocks.
Five percent yields are possible with the right REITs.
Ditch low-yield stocks for these high-income alternatives.
pack.reddit_headline: 1% dividend yields: is the S&P 500 a lost cause for passive income?
